title: Library Assignment Order
match_hints: if it is not possible output -1
notes: Choose the smallest studentID when multiple students can finish.
A student can finish when current available books cover required minus issued.
After finishing, the student returns all currently issued books.

title: Case-Insensitive Substring Count
match_hints: disregard the case of the letters
number of occurrences of the second string in the first string
if no occurrence of Sub is found in Parent then print 0
notes: Convert both strings to the same case before matching.
The archived solution counts overlapping matches.
If sub is longer than parent, the answer is 0.

title: Dictionary Subsequence Filter
match_hints: given a dictionary of N words and a master word
formed by deleting some characters of the given master word
in the order that the words appear in the dictionary
notes: A valid dictionary word must be a subsequence of the master word.
The archived solution uses character positions plus binary search.
If no word matches, print -1.

title: Palindromic Sales List
match_hints: add together any two consecutive elements
convert the given list into a palindromic list of maximum length
notes: Maximum length is equivalent to minimum number of merges.
Merge the side with the smaller running sum until both sides are equal.

title: Top-Left Submatrix Product Count
match_hints: contain the top left element of matrix A
product less than or equal to K
notes: Only prefix rectangles need to be considered.
Any prefix rectangle containing zero has product 0 and is valid when K >= 0.
The archived solution caps large products at K + 1.

title: Minimum Cable Length
match_hints: systems are placed in a row
distance of the systems from the first system
notes: OFF systems before the first ON and after the last ON form forced chains.
Between two ON systems, the optimal cost is segment span minus the largest adjacent gap.

title: Smallest Registration Permutation
sample_output: smallest digit permutation without leading zero
notes: Positive numbers use ascending order with the first non-zero digit moved to the front.
Negative numbers use descending order on the absolute digits, then restore the negative sign.

title: Remove Duplicates Preserve Order
match_hints: same order as they appear in the input list
notes: Keep the first occurrence of each value.
Use a set for O(1) duplicate checks.

title: LRU Cache Misses
notes: Use recency order to simulate LRU.
On a miss with full cache, evict the least recently used page.
